action: Reverse Troy redirect — make /outpatient/troy the canonical (it's the URL Google indexes)
why: /outpatient/troy is the Troy page that ranks — 438 clicks / pos 4.66 / 48.8K imp on 365d. But right now that URL 301s to /outpatient-rehab-troy-ny, which gets 0 traffic. Google is indexing the URL we're redirecting away from. The page that gets traffic should be the destination, not the source.
how: Step 1: curl -I both URLs to confirm the current redirect direction. Step 2: reverse it — /outpatient-rehab-troy-ny should 301 to /outpatient/troy. Step 3: clean up the sitemap to list only the canonical URL. Step 4: GSC URL Inspection on the canonical URL to confirm Google sees it.
acceptance: Canonical Troy URL is /outpatient/troy and returns 200; legacy 301s to it; sitemap clean

action: Audit /outpatient/troy post-SP-01 → rebuild only if page content is thin

If that page already has substantive content (intake form, services, NAP, FAQ), the redirect fix alone should recover CTR — no rebuild needed. If it's thin (because the team built a new page elsewhere and left the canonical empty), rebuild using the standard template. Per §1c competitor churn, Troy is gaining +107 top-10 cells across 5 queries but Whitney M Young (+155) + Hope House Outpatient (+99) are pushing into 'outpatient rehab near me' top-3.

Step 2 (IF thin): rebuild per Conifer_Park_Outpatient_Pages_Plan.xlsx row=troy. 1,200-1,400 words. Schema: MedicalClinic + Place with Troy NAP (post-LG-TR-01 2431 vs 2435 resolution). Inline intake form. Step 3 (IF already substantive): light polish only — schema add + internal link cluster reinforcement.
acceptance: Audit outcome documented in Outpatient_Pages_Plan.xlsx row=troy; if rebuild executed, page live + LD scans confirm 'outpatient rehab near me' top-3 floor ≥35 cells per §1b
